title: Lampropappus turbinellus
intro: Lampropappus turbinellus, also known as Turbinella, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Bioactive constituents of Turbinella include sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds, which are thought to contribute to its health benefits. Traditionally, Turbinella is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Lampropappus turbinellus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Lampropappus and is further classified as a member of the tribe Anthemideae. The species is closely related to the genus Chrysocephalum and is sometimes included in that genus. The classification of Lampropappus turbinellus is subject to ongoing research and some taxonomists may group it differently. More information on its classification is available in botanical literature and databases.
